comments.body: Well the dependency you added isn't in Maven Central, so obviously it won't be resolved from there. There is some 0.5 version available so not sure what version or dependency you are trying to download.
The latest version of that dependency seems to be 0.5.7: github.com/miho/JCSG/releases
I tried 0.5.7 and got the same results.  What is the correct location of the dependency?
Is com.github.miho:jcsg:2.1.0 from ChatGPT? There is eu.mihosoft.vrl.jcsg:jcsg:0.5.7 in Maven Central, but I don't know if that's the dependency you're really looking for.
The repository README would seem to indicate you are meant to compile and assemble the jar yourself.

It seemed to work with eu.mihosoft.vrl.jcsg:jcsg:0.5.7 and loaded the jar files into the project but it didn't pull in some of the other jars it references like JavaFX.  I'm trying to find the scraps now.

answers.body:
This may help. I cloned JFXScad and installed that project in Eclipse 2025-09, Java 21, Windows 11.  A './gradlew clean build' was done in Powershell, gradle-version 8.10. A run on JFXScad.java shows an example, and one Sample can be selected per run (toggling between Samples generates an exception).

gradle-wrapper-properties file:

#Sun May 28 15:46:30 CEST 2017
distributionBase=GRADLE_USER_HOME
distributionPath=wrapper/dists
zipStoreBase=GRADLE_USER_HOME
zipStorePath=wrapper/dists
distributionUrl=https\://services.gradle.org/distributions/gradle-8.10-bin.zip


build.gradle file:

plugins {
    id 'java'
    id 'eclipse'
    id 'idea'
    id 'org.openjfx.javafxplugin' version '0.1.0'
}
group = 'eu.mihosoft.jfxscad'
version = '1.0.0'
java {
    sourceCompatibility = JavaVersion.VERSION_21
    targetCompatibility = JavaVersion.VERSION_21
}
repositories {
    mavenCentral()
}
dependencies {
    implementation 'org.fxmisc.richtext:richtextfx:0.6.9'
    implementation 'org.codehaus.groovy:groovy:2.3.7'
    implementation 'org.controlsfx:controlsfx:8.0.6'
    implementation 'commons-io:commons-io:2.4'
    implementation 'eu.mihosoft.vrl.jcsg:jcsg:0.5.2'
    testImplementation 'junit:junit:4.10'
}
javafx {
    version = "17"
    modules = [ 'javafx.controls', 'javafx.fxml', 'javafx.swing' ]
}
tasks.withType(JavaCompile).configureEach {
    options.encoding = 'UTF-8'
}
jar {
    manifest {
        attributes(
            'Main-Class': 'eu.mihosoft.vrl.fxscad.JFXScad'
        )
    }
    duplicatesStrategy = DuplicatesStrategy.EXCLUDE
    from {
        configurations.runtimeClasspath.collect { it.isDirectory() ? it : zipTree(it) }
    }
}

title: Cannot find the gradle.build for JCSG
body:
I am trying to get "Java implementation of BSP based CSG (Constructive Solid Geometry)" and all its dependencies into an Eclipse Java project. Google and ChatGPT give different values for the gradle dependency. They all fail with "unresolved dependency". How do I do it?

I tried multiple domains and versions with something like this for build.gradle:

plugins {
  id 'java'
}

repositories {
  mavenCentral()
}

dependencies {
  implementation 'com.github.miho:jcsg:2.1.0'
}

The form would not let me include it with the proper spacing.
